> scratch_utf8.txt:1255:the Spring Security `CustomAuthenticationSuccessHandler` redirects you to the saved request 
URL.\n4. **404 Error**: 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1256:Since there is no actual file or controller handling `/login.css.map`, the server returns a 
**404 Not Found** 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1257:page.\n\n---\n\n### How to resolve this:\nThere are two ways you can fix this in the 
code:\n\n* **Option A**: Add 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1258:source map extensions (`/**/*.map`, `/*.map`) to the ignored request configuration in 
[SecurityConfig.java](file:///c:/
  scratch_utf8.txt:1259:Users/sibi/Desktop/Stratroom-Source/stratroom-web/src/main/java/com/estrat/web/security/Securit
yConfig.java):\n  
  scratch_utf8.txt:1260:```java\n  http.ignoring().antMatchers(\"/**/*.map\");\n  ```\n* **Option B**: Remove the 
source map comment from the 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1261:CSS stylesheet in 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1262:[index.jsp](file:///c:/Users/sibi/Desktop/Stratroom-Source/stratroom-web/src/main/webapp/view/p
ages/index.jsp) (Lines 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1263:211 and 216):\n  ```css\n  /* Remove this comment */\n  /*# sourceMappingURL=login.css.map 
*/\n  
  scratch_utf8.txt:1264:```","thinking":"**Analyzing the Error**\n\nI'm currently focused on dissecting the \"Not 
Found\" error. The 404 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1265:status, in conjunction with the attempted access of a `.css.map` file, suggests a potential 
misconfiguration or 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1266:missing resource in the application's CSS mapping. The Whitelabel Error Page further confirms 
this. I am now exploring 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1267:possible causes for the file's unavailability, such as a deployment issue or incorrect 
pathing.\n\n\n**Investigating 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1268:the Navigation**\n\nI've made a breakthrough. I now understand that the request for 
`login.css.map` isn't a direct 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1269:consequence of an error, but rather a side effect of having the browser's DevTools open. It's 
automatically requesting 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1270:source maps, as indicated by the comment `/*# sourceMappingURL=login.css.map */` within the 
CSS files. I am now trying 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1271:to determine why the browser's address bar is navigated to that specific URL. I'm exploring 
potential causes such as a 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1272:URL parameter, a form submission, or a Spring Security redirect.\n\n\n**Pinpointing the 
Redirect**\n\nI've uncovered 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1273:the root cause: Spring Security's redirect logic is tripping over the browser's source map 
requests. The crucial piece 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1274:is that the unauthenticated request for `login.css.map` gets saved. This then causes 
> scratch_utf8.txt:1275:`CustomAuthenticationSuccessHandler` to redirect to this invalid URL upon successful login, 
leading to the 404 error. 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1276:The issue arises when the request for the source map, requested by developer tools, is not 
ignored by `WebSecurity` 
  scratch_utf8.txt:1277:ignore config, resulting in Spring Security saving it as the target for redirection.\n\n\n"}
  scratch_utf8.txt:1278:
